methods.name: Peer-Median

methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Pyka is a Series B autonomous drone company founded by Michael Norcia, Chuma Ogunwole, Kyle Moore, and Nathan White. The target operates in the Drones & Autonomous sector alongside 8 private peers including Elroy Air, Zipline, Joby Aviation, Nuro, and Archer Aviation. No public comparables exist in this narrow subsector.

Of the two valuation methods assessed, only stage-progression proved applicable. The peer-median approach was inaccessible due to insufficient narrow peer coverage: while 5 same-sector companies exist, filtering by stage, adjacent-stage, or valuation tier yielded fewer than 3 datapoints required for robust triangulation. Stage-progression applied a 1.9x step-up multiplier to Pyka's $40M total raised, implying a Series B valuation of $76M.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $65M to $87M, with $76M as the median point. This range reflects conservative (low) and optimistic (high) bookends around the stage-progression result, the sole applicable method. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the 1.9x multiplier for early-stage autonomous hardware companies and Pyka's positioning relative to the broader peer set. A robust peer-median cross-check cannot be performed given the limited comparable universe; deeper diligence on unit economics, deployment traction, and competitive differentiation would strengthen conviction.
